title: Malty Dense Moroccan Chocolate Tagine
description: A rich and festive Moroccan dessert featuring dark chocolate, double-rise-bread, and spices.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together flour, brown sugar, cocoa powder, and salt.
Add in the softened butter and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Cut the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Place the balls onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 15-20 minutes or until firm to the touch.
Let the chocolate bread cool completely on a wire rack.
Cut the bread into 1-inch cubes and set aside.
In a large saucepan, melt the dark chocolate chips over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add in the granulated sugar, vanilla extract, cinnamon, and ginger, and stir until combined.
Add in the bread cubes and stir until well coated in the chocolate mixture.
Transfer the tagine to a serving dish and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to set.
Optional: garnish with additional dark chocolate shavings or chopped nuts if desired.
